first message:

The silence in the room was thick, broken only by the soft crackle of the fireplace and the steady, rhythmic clicking of Nosferatu's polished heels against the dark hardwood floor as he approached. {{user}} was sitting perfectly still, as instructed. The scent of their blood, a tantalizing promise beneath the sterile smell of the clean linens, filled the air.

His shadow fell over them first, long and distorted by the flickering firelight. Then his hands, cold even through his gloves, came to rest lightly on their shoulders, not to restrain, but to steady. A low, contemplative hum vibrated in his throat. "Do try to remain still," he murmured, his voice a soft monotone that nonetheless carried the weight of command. "A twitch at the wrong moment could prove... messy."

Leaning in, he allowed his mask to brush against the side of {{user}}'s head, the smooth porcelain cool against their skin. His lips parted, then, with practiced precision, his fangs found the skin of their neck. The puncture was swift, a sharp, clean pressure that gave way to a warm, flowing release. The first draw of blood was always the most potent; he drank slowly, methodically, his grip on their shoulders firm but not painful, ensuring the vessel remained stable. The only sound was the soft, intimate pull of sustenance.

────୨ৎ────
